title: SQL and Stored Procedures Support
text: SQL and Stored Procedures Support For SQL-enabled databases, you can use the sql ProcScript instruction and SQL Editor to send SQL instructions to the database. Some databases also support stored procedures. The following table shows the functionality supported by the database connector SQL Support—indicates whether you can use the sql ProcScript command and SQL Editor to send SQL instructions to the database. For more information, see sql and SQL Editor . Stored Procedures—indicates whether the connector can generate stored procedures for the most basic I/O operations. Service Stored Procedures—indicates whether the connector supports service stored procedures, which enable stored procedures to be executed using the activate ProcScript statement. For more information, see Service Stored Procedures (SSPs) and Define Signatures for Service Stored Procedures . Table 1. SQL and Stored Procedures Support Connector SQL Support? Stored Procedures? Service Stored Procedures? Details DB2 Yes Yes Yes For more information, see SQL Support on DB2 and DB2 Service Stored Procedures . Informix Yes Yes Yes For more information, see SQL Support on Informix , Informix Stored Procedures and Informix Service Stored Procedures . LDAP No No No MS SQL Server Yes Yes Yes For more information, see SQL Support for Microsoft SQL Server , Stored Procedures on Microsoft SQL Server and Service Stored Procedures on Microsoft SQL Server . MySQL Yes No Yes For more information, see SQL Support on MySQL and MySQL Service Stored Procedures . ODBC Yes No No For more information, see SQL Support on ODBC . Oracle Yes Yes Yes For more information, see SQL Support on Oracle and Oracle Stored Packages . PostgreSQL Yes Yes Yes For more information, see SQL Support for PostgreSQL , Stored Procedures on PostgreSQL and Service Stored Procedures on PostgreSQL . Sequential No No No SQLite Yes No No For more information, see SQL Support on SQLite . Sybase Yes Yes Yes For more information, see SQL Support on Sybase . For more information, see Sybase Stored Procedures . TXT No No No Related Links sql Pass a DML statement to the specified DBMS path. Service Stored Procedures (SSPs) Uniface supports the execution of DBMS stored procedures by using the activate statement to address a Stored Procedure Component defined with the Signature Editor. This concept is referred to within Uniface as Service Stored Procedures. Define Signatures for Service Stored Procedures Create a signature for a service stored procedure, in which one or more stored procedures are represented to Uniface as operations.
